title: logOut() can leave a stale session if save() fails before regenerate()
body: SessionManager.prototype.logOut deletes req.session[this._key].user, then calls req.session.save(), and only calls req.session.regenerate() inside save's callback. If save() errors out, regenerate() never runs, so the old session ID (now missing its user) persists with an error surfaced to the caller instead of being cleaned up.

title: use() silently registers a strategy under name 'undefined'
body: Authenticator.prototype.use infers the name from strategy.name when no explicit name is passed. If a custom Strategy subclass forgets to set this.name in its constructor, use() throws 'Authentication strategies must have a name' only if name is falsy -- but some values coerce to a truthy string 'undefined', letting a badly-named strategy register silently and fail later at authenticate('undefined') time with a confusing error.

title: What does keepSessionInfo actually preserve during login/logout?
body: Both logIn and logOut merge `prevSession` back into `req.session` after regenerate() when `options.keepSessionInfo` is set, but it's not documented which session keys are expected to survive vs. which are meant to be dropped by regeneration. Could this be clarified with an example?

title: Calling initialize() twice on the same app isn't documented
body: We accidentally mounted passport.initialize() middleware twice (once in a shared middleware module, once in app.js) and didn't get any warning, just subtly duplicated request.login attachment. Should initialize.js guard against or at least document double-initialization?
